description: Topic taxonomy with weights (0-100). Higher weight = higher priority in Pulse. Based on revealed preferences from homeeconomics.substack.com.

core.label: Core
core.description: Always feature these. Dominate the briefing.

occasional.label: Occasional
occasional.description: Include when interesting, not a focus.

excluded.label: Excluded
excluded.description: Avoid unless they tie back to a core topic.

housing_prices.label: Housing Prices
housing_prices.description: Home price data, indices, forecasts, regional patterns, undervalued markets.
examples: WSJ: 'Home prices fell in 18 of 20 Case-Shiller cities, biggest drop since 2012'
@LoganMohtashami: 'Existing home sales just posted the weakest March since 1995. Affordability remains the binding constraint.'
Resiclub newsletter: 'Zillow now forecasts -1.8% national home price growth in 2026, down from +2.4% last month'

housing_geography.label: Housing Geography
housing_geography.description: Metro-by-metro analysis, suburbs vs cities, regional booms (Sun Belt, Atlanta, Bay Area, NYC, Florida, etc.).
examples: Bloomberg CityLab: 'Why Phoenix exurbs are growing four times faster than the urban core'
@JedKolko: 'Austin home prices down 12% from peak, but inventory still 3x pre-COVID. The boom's hangover.'
NYT: 'Tampa lost more residents than it gained for the first time since 2018'

housing_inventory.label: Housing Inventory & Supply
housing_inventory.description: Inventory levels, listings, days on market, months of supply, supply dynamics.
examples: @calculatedrisk: 'Active listings now 32% above 2019 levels nationally. Florida and Texas up 60%+.'
Redfin: 'Months of supply hits 5.2 in Austin, highest since 2011'
WSJ: 'For the first time in three years, more sellers are cutting prices than raising them'

mortgage_rates.label: Mortgage Rates & Lock-in
mortgage_rates.description: Mortgage rates, lock-in effect, refinancing, rate impacts on the housing market.
examples: Bloomberg: '30-year mortgage drops to 5.95%, lowest since October'
@LoganMohtashami: 'Below 6% is the magic level. We're seeing purchase apps tick up for the first time in months.'
MBA: 'Refinance applications surge 18% week-over-week as rates dip'

affordability.label: Affordability & Buyer Math
affordability.description: Price-to-income, rent burden, who can afford, buy vs rent calculations, first-time buyer math.
examples: Harvard JCHS: '22.6 million renter households are cost-burdened — a record'
@arpitrage: 'Median home in SF requires household income of $360K to qualify. Median household income is $130K.'
Zillow: 'Income required to afford a typical US home has nearly doubled since 2020'

homeownership_demographics.label: Homeownership & Demographics
homeownership_demographics.description: Generational gaps in homeownership, millennials, first-time buyers, household formation, family dynamics around housing.
examples: NAR: 'Median age of first-time homebuyer hits 38, up from 31 in 2010'
@kaerdmann: 'Millennials own 25% of US housing wealth despite being 22% of the population. The gap is closing faster than people think.'
Pew Research: 'Share of Americans 25-34 living with parents at 18%, highest since 1940'

construction_supply.label: Construction & Building
construction_supply.description: Housing starts, permits, builder activity, construction employment as a leading indicator.
examples: Census: 'Housing starts fell 7.2% in March to a 1.32M annual pace'
AIA: 'Architecture Billings Index drops to 44.5 — six straight months of contraction'
@RickPalaciosJr: 'New home sales in Texas down 18% YoY. Builders are pulling back hard.'

immigration_housing.label: Immigration & Housing
immigration_housing.description: Immigration's effect on housing demand, labor supply for construction, demographic effects.
examples: FT: 'Trump deportation push will hit construction labor force hardest, builders warn'
@aarmlovi: 'Half of US construction workforce is immigrant. Cut immigration, cut housing supply.'
Brookings: 'Without immigration, US household formation would have been negative in 2025'

ai_and_housing.label: AI × Housing
ai_and_housing.description: Where AI intersects housing: tech worker geography, AI-driven demand, AI in real estate.
examples: Bloomberg: 'AI hiring boom drives Bay Area rents up 12% year-over-year'
@JedKolko: 'AI engineer salaries are pushing median Bay Area home prices past $2M for the first time'
TechCrunch: 'Anthropic, OpenAI, and xAI account for 40% of new SF leases above $5M'

happiness_wellbeing.label: Happiness & Wellbeing
happiness_wellbeing.description: Happiness research, social outcomes, anglosphere unhappiness, wellbeing trends, cultural shifts.
examples: World Happiness Report: 'US falls to 24th, lowest ever. Young Americans drag the average down.'
@cremieuxrecueil: 'The collapse in self-reported happiness among under-30s is now the biggest cohort effect in postwar data.'
NYT: 'Loneliness epidemic gets worse: half of young Americans report feeling lonely most days'

housing_policy.label: Housing Policy & YIMBY
housing_policy.description: Zoning, YIMBY/NIMBY, rent control, public housing, ADUs, upzoning, housing politics.
examples: @AlecStapp: 'Texas just passed the most ambitious upzoning bill of any red state. Statewide ADU legalization, parking minimums abolished.'
Vox: 'Why Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze is dividing NYC progressives'
Bloomberg: 'California's SB 9 has produced 200 ADUs in three years. Critics call it a failure.'

ai_general.label: AI General
ai_general.description: AI tools, AI industry developments, AI productivity, model releases.
examples: @emollick: 'Claude 4.5 Sonnet just hit 92% on SWE-bench. The capability gains keep accelerating.'
Stratechery: 'Anthropic's 1M context window changes the unit economics of code generation'
@simonw: 'OpenAI shipped a new agentic mode. It's the first one that doesn't immediately fall over on real tasks.'

tech_geography.label: Tech Geography
tech_geography.description: Where tech jobs are, Silicon Valley dominance, tech hiring/firing by metro, hubs vs satellites.
examples: Apricitas Economics: 'California's tech share fell to 16%, lowest since 2013. Texas and Florida are absorbing the displacement.'
SignalFire: 'Bay Area now hosts 65% of all AI engineers — up from 52% pre-ChatGPT'
WSJ: 'Tech layoffs hit 50,000 in Q1, mostly concentrated in San Francisco and Seattle'

cities_urbanism.label: Cities & Urbanism
cities_urbanism.description: City policy, transit, urban form, walkability, downtown revitalization.
examples: @mnolangray: 'Atlanta added 200 miles of bike lanes in five years. The transformation is real.'
CityLab: 'Phoenix light rail expansion stalls as ridership flatlines'
Strong Towns: 'The hidden cost of one-way streets in mid-sized American cities'

demographics_general.label: Demographics & Migration
demographics_general.description: Birth rates, household formation, internal migration, population trends.
examples: @lymanstoneky: 'US fertility hit 1.62 in 2025 — a new record low. Marriage rates explain ~70% of the variance.'
Census: 'Domestic migration to Sun Belt slows for second consecutive year'
Pew: 'Population growth in 2025 was 0.4%, lowest since 1937 outside of pandemic years'

commercial_real_estate.label: Commercial Real Estate
examples: WSJ: 'San Francisco office vacancy hits 35%, the highest of any major US city'
@jayparsons: 'Multifamily delinquencies climbing in Sun Belt — early warning sign for the asset class'
Bloomberg: 'Manhattan office-to-residential conversions slow as construction costs surge'

climate_insurance.label: Climate & Insurance
climate_insurance.description: Property insurance crisis, climate risk to housing, natural disasters.
examples: NYT: 'Florida insurance market in crisis as State Farm exits'
First Street Foundation: '12 million homes face wildfire risk not reflected in insurance pricing'
WSJ: 'FEMA flood maps expand by 40% in Southeast — hidden insurance shock for millions'

cultural_lifestyle.label: Cultural & Lifestyle
cultural_lifestyle.description: Lifestyle commentary, time use, cultural shifts, dual-career families.
examples: Atlantic: 'The collapse of the second job — and what it means for two-earner families'
@DKThomp: 'Americans now spend an average 7.5 hours/day on screens. Up 50% in a decade.'
Vox: 'Why working from home killed business casual'

politics_housing.label: Politics & Housing
politics_housing.description: Housing politics, voter dynamics on housing, election housing positions.
examples: Politico: 'Housing affordability dominates the 2026 NYC mayoral race'
@AlecStapp: 'YIMBYs went 8-for-10 in last week's state legislative races. The movement is winning.'
FiveThirtyEight: 'Voters under 35 say housing is their top issue, ahead of inflation and immigration'

pure_fed_macro.label: Pure Fed / Macro / Inflation / Jobs
pure_fed_macro.description: Fed policy, FOMC, inflation reports (CPI/PCE/PPI), jobs reports, payrolls, unemployment, GDP, retail sales — UNLESS explicitly tied to housing implications.
examples: Reuters: 'March CPI rises 3.2% YoY, beating consensus'
@NickTimiraos: 'Powell's testimony today suggests the Fed is in no rush to cut rates'
BLS: 'Nonfarm payrolls add 215K in March, unemployment rate ticks up to 4.1%'

markets_finance.label: Markets & Finance
markets_finance.description: Stock markets, bond yields, dollar moves, market commentary, broader finance.
examples: CNBC: 'S&P 500 closes at record high, up 0.8%'
@LizAnnSonders: '10-year Treasury yield breaks below 4% for first time since November'
Bloomberg: 'Dollar index falls to 6-month low as traders price in Fed cuts'

international.label: International (non-US)
international.description: Non-US housing markets, international economic news (UK, Canada, Europe, Asia).
examples: FT: 'UK house prices fall for fourth consecutive month'
Reuters: 'China property crisis deepens as Country Garden reports record loss'
Globe and Mail: 'Toronto real estate sales plunge 22% as buyers stay sidelined'

tech_general.label: Tech General (non-AI, non-housing)
tech_general.description: Generic tech news that doesn't connect to AI or housing or labor geography.
examples: The Verge: 'Apple unveils new iPhone Pro with redesigned camera system'
TechCrunch: 'Meta's Q1 earnings beat expectations, ad revenue up 14%'
Bloomberg: 'Tesla recalls 1M vehicles to fix software bug'
